channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A trillion dollar question: Will SpaceX's Starship launch go well?
item.description: Ahead of a much anticipated IPO, SpaceX is carrying out a critical test of its giant, stainless steel rocket. Investors will be watching closely.
item.content:encoded:
Ahead of a much anticipated IPO, SpaceX is carrying out a critical test of its giant, stainless steel rocket. Investors will be watching closely.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Researchers say the Trump administration is finding new ways to punish science
item.description: Even with federal grants largely restored, scientists say the Trump administration is still preventing those funds from reaching them. The consequences, they say, are already becoming clear.
item.content:encoded:
Even with federal grants largely restored, scientists say the Trump administration is still preventing those funds from reaching them. The consequences, they say, are already becoming clear.

(Image credit: Jodi Hilton for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: COVID is shaping Americans' reaction to Ebola and hantavirus
item.description: Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.
item.content:encoded:
Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: A guide to converting your lawn into a wildlife friendly garden
item.description: Turning your grass into a garden isn't as complicated as you think, but it will take time and effort. This step-by-step guide breaks down the process, from killing your lawn to picking plants to grow.
item.content:encoded:
Turning your grass into a garden isn't as complicated as you think, but it will take time and effort. This step-by-step guide breaks down the process, from killing your lawn to picking plants to grow.

(Image credit: Celia Llopis-Jepsen/KCUR)
item.dc:creator: Celia Llopis-Jepsen

item.title: As floods get worse, Britain tries a new solution: beavers
item.description: About 400 years ago, beavers were hunted to extinction across Britain. Now they're being reintroduced as little climate warriors, as communities harness their dam-building skills to mitigate flooding.
item.content:encoded:
About 400 years ago, beavers were hunted to extinction across Britain. Now they're being reintroduced as little climate warriors, as communities harness their dam-building skills to mitigate flooding.

(Image credit: Dan Kitwood)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO plans reveal blockbuster spending on rockets and AI
item.description: The company is on track to pull off the largest IPO in history — making CEO Elon Musk even wealthier.
item.content:encoded:
The company is on track to pull off the largest IPO in history — making CEO Elon Musk even wealthier.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: Bees have coexisted with us for over a millennium. Their name remains a mystery
item.description: Its name is short — like its size — but the bee is one of Earth's most important and busy creatures.
item.content:encoded:
Its name is short — like its size — but the bee is one of Earth's most important and busy creatures.

(Image credit: Alan Nakkash for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Can't keep a habit? This comic shares a proven formula to make it stick
item.description: In this illustrated guide, behavioral scientist BJ Fogg breaks down his Tiny Habits framework to help you rethink your approach to starting (or restarting) a habit.
item.content:encoded: In this illustrated guide, behavioral scientist BJ Fogg breaks down his Tiny Habits framework to help you rethink your approach to starting (or restarting) a habit.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: To revive an extinct bird, you first need an artificial egg
item.description: Colossal Biosciences, a Texas company trying to bring extinct species back to life, reports creating artificial eggs that would be necessary to revive extinct birds such as the dodo.
item.content:encoded:
Colossal Biosciences, a Texas company trying to bring extinct species back to life, reports creating artificial eggs that would be necessary to revive extinct birds such as the dodo.

(Image credit: Colossal Biosciences)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Some plants have a genetic superpower that may help them survive a cataclysm
item.description: Get ready for a biology lesson. Certain plants have extra sets of chromosomes. And it turns out, it's a useful trait for a species facing a dramatic event like climate change.
item.content:encoded:
Get ready for a biology lesson. Certain plants have extra sets of chromosomes. And it turns out, it's a useful trait for a species facing a dramatic event like climate change.

(Image credit: Wolfgang Kaehler/LightRocket)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel
